2004-03-01.title: At Halfway Point of Milosevic Trial, Prosecutor Is Confident
2004-03-01.abstract: Interview with war crimes prosecutor Carla Del Ponte, who has rested her case against former Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic; she predicts he will be convicted; says she offered abundant evidence of war crimes and crimes against humanity in Bosnia, Croatia and Serbia's Kosovo Province; some experts question whether she has proved him guilty of genocide in Bosnia; photo (M)

2004-03-01.title: Schröder and Bush find mutual interest : U.S.-German reasons to reconcile
2004-03-01.abstract: BERLIN:—  Apart from the ruffles-and-flourishes overstatement of a too grandly named joint declaration — A German-American Alliance for the 21st Century — the White House meeting between President George W. Bush and Chancellor Gerhard Schröder last week opened a pathway to what, at the least, are more temperate and cooperative trans-Atlantic relations.

2004-03-01.title: Russia Seizes 2 Athletes In a Dispute With Qatar
2004-03-01.abstract: Qatar reports Russia has detained two wrestlers from Qatar as they traveled via Russia to Olympic qualifying tournament in Serbia; move adds to diplomatic dispute over Qatar's arrest of two Russian secret agents for assassination of exiled Chechen leader Zelimkhan Yandarbiyev in Feb 13 bomb blast in Doha; Russia has repeatedly demanded release of agents; Qatar's government-owned newspaper scores Russian retaliation (M)

2004-03-01.title: German Conservatives Hand Socialists a Drubbing in Local Hamburg Voting
2004-03-01.abstract: German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's party suffers decisive loss in local elections, Hamburg, to main conservative opposition party, which gains some 20 percentage points from 2001 election; vote, seen as strong sign of voter dissatisfaction with Schroder government, should enable opposition to govern city without any coalition partners; numerous local contests in next several months are seen as informal referendum on Schroder's economic reforms; local factors in Hamburg vote cited (M)

2004-03-01.title: Iraq Oil Industry Reviving as Output Nears Prewar Levels
2004-03-01.abstract: American and Iraqi officials say Iraq's oil industry, in remarkable turnaround, is now producing and exporting 2.3 million to 2.5 million barrels a day, close to prewar level of 2.8 million barrels; coalition official Robert McKee, leading American figure in drive to restore oil fields, says oil exports could give Iraq $14 billion this year, up from $5 billion last year; challenges facing oil industry after it reverts to Iraqi control noted (M)

2004-03-02.title: Military Still Able to Respond to New Crisis
2004-03-02.abstract: News analysis says that decision to send up to 2,000 marines to help stabilize Haiti shows that US military can still intervene in crises large and small, even while so many ground troops are involved in major missions; photo; sending marines to Haiti is not especially complicated, but it is one more unexpected commitment that Bush administration is not eager to embrace in election year; Sec Donald Rumsfeld and Gen Richard B Myers pointedly decline to say if marines would intervene to stop looting or protect Haitian civilians (M)

2004-03-02.title: Israel May Ask Egypt to Help Secure Gaza After a Pullout
2004-03-02.abstract: Senior Israeli official says Prime Min Ariel Sharon wants Egypt to help secure Gaza Strip after proposed withdrawal of Israeli forces and settlers; says US should not be concerned about Hamas takeover there; also says Israel plans to shift military from large parts of area west of Jordan River to buffer zone in western part of West Bank, to protect settlements; likens plan to parking place while waiting for Palestinians to negotiate boundaries of its state; top officials brief Sec Colin Powell and Condoleezza Rice in Washington (M)

2004-03-02.title: At Least 25 Die in Attack on Shiites in Pakistan
2004-03-02.abstract: Unidentified gunmen lobbed grenades and sprayed bullets at a procession of Shiite Muslim worshippers in the city of Quetta in southwestern Pakistan today.

2004-03-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Chechen Rebel Killed
2004-03-02.abstract: Russian Deputy Prosecutor General Sergei N Fridinsky and former Chechan rebels identify body of rebel commander Ruslan Gelayev, who had been reported killed in recent clash with Russian border troops in Dagestan (Russia); photo (S)

2004-03-02.title: This Baghdad Gunfire Celebrates Love, Not War
2004-03-02.abstract: Wedding of Shayma Hamid, 21, and Ali Dakhil Alwai, 36, is one of many in Baghdad for young couples who were forced to wait out most turbulent moments of Iraq war; photos (M)

2004-03-03.title: At Least 143 Die in Attacks At Two Sacred Sites in Iraq
2004-03-03.abstract: Suicide bombers and other attackers detonate mortars, grenades and roadside bombs among crowds of Shiite Muslims gathered for holy day observances at mosques in Baghdad and Karbala, killing at least 143 people; dead reportedly include pilgrims from Iran; death toll is highest in Iraq since United States-led invasion; much of angry reaction to attacks is focused on occupation; American officials suspect Abu Mussab al-Zarqawi, Sunni militant who wrote to senior Al Qaeda leaders seeking help in starting 'sectarian war' by attacking Shiites to spur counterattack by them on Sunnis; one of four suicide bombers is arrested when his explosive-laden vest fails to detonate; US intelligence official sees no link between attacks and attack on Shiites marking same holy day, Ashura, in Quetta, Pakistan; maps; chronology of attacks against Muslim targets; photos (M)

2004-03-03.title: Today Carinthia, Tomorrow Vienna?
2004-03-03.abstract: Jorg Haider, right-wing nationalist whose presence in Austrian government prompted European Union to impose sanctions on Austria five years ago, is campaigning hard in advance of local elections in Carinthia, province where he is governor; Byzantine quality of Austrian coalition politics could keep Haider and his Freedom Party in office in province even if he loses popular vote; his failure to remain governor, post chosen by state legislature, would likely mean effective demise of party, once specter haunting all of Europe, as force in Austria; he has been serving relatively quietly as governor since fading from national scene two years ago; is presumed to control party, still part of nation's governing coalition, from behind scenes; his bent for outrageous statements has made him one of Austria's most recognizable politicians; map; photo (M)

2004-03-03.title: Blackmail Plot to Plant Bombs on French Railroads Disclosed
2004-03-03.abstract: PARIS, March 3 — In a bizarre blackmail plot against the French state, an unknown terrorist entity has threatened to explode bombs throughout the French railway system unless it is paid more than $5 million, the Interior Ministry said today.

2004-03-03.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-03-03.abstract: NORTHERN IRELAND: Unionists Quit Talks; BRITAIN: Coke Questioned On Bottled Tap; BRITAIN: Weapons Inquiry To Continue; RUSSIA: 7 From GuantÁNamo Charged; ITALY: Power To The Islanders;.

2004-03-03.title: Dutch Arrest 2 Africans Suspected of War Crimes
2004-03-03.abstract: Dutch police, who are screening asylum seekers for possible human rights violations, report arresting two former military officers, one from Rwanda and one from Congo, for war crimes in their home nations; expect Rwandan Ephrem Setako will be extradited to be tried by United Nations tribunal based in Tanzania; say Netherlands, as signer of 1987 Convention Against Torture, must try Congolese refugee on rape and torture charges under Dutch law (M)

2004-03-04.title: Powell and Aide Questioned On Haiti by Panel's Skeptics
2004-03-04.abstract: Secretary of State Colin L. Powell and his top aide for Latin America faced fierce questioning on Wednesday from lawmakers who rejected the administration's claims that President Jean-Bertrand Aristide of Haiti had resigned of his own free will.    At a hearing dominated by Democratic members of the Congressional Black Caucus, Roger F. Noriega, the assistant secretary of state for Western Hemisphere affairs, was denounced as insolent and misguided, and faced derisive laughter, as he testified that the United States had not forced Mr. Aristide from office.

2004-03-04.title: The little middle:hipsters make way for the narrow waist
2004-03-04.abstract: PARIS—  Just when it seemed relegated to style history, the waist is back at the epicenter of fashion. Not since the 1980s, when broad shoulders empowered women for work and made waists seem narrow by comparison, has so much attention been directed at the midriff.

2004-03-04.title: The Collections / Paris : Dior is big, bright and bold
2004-03-04.abstract: Wednesday should have been V for victory day at Dior. First affiliated luxury company LVMH (Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton) announced a 30 percent rise in profits for 2003, so that Bernard Arnault sat front row with dark and dashing Liz Hurley and blonde Rebecca Romijn-Stamos looking like the proverbial cat with the cream.

2004-03-04.title: Bomb Damages New Baghdad Phone Center; Sabotage Hinders Rebuilding
2004-03-04.abstract: A bomb ripped into Iraq's main international phone exchange on Wednesday, killing one man and cutting service to hundreds of thousands of Iraqis just days after being restored.     Preliminary reports suggested that the man who was killed, an Iraqi worker recently hired to lay a roof in the telecommunications compound, may have planted the bomb.

2004-03-04.title: Researchers Retract a Study Linking Autism To Vaccination
2004-03-04.abstract: Ten of the 13 scientists who produced a 1998 study linking a childhood vaccine to several cases of autism retracted their conclusion yesterday.    In a statement to be published in the March 6 issue of The Lancet, a British medical journal, the researchers conceded that they did not have enough evidence at the time to tie the measles, mumps and rubella vaccine, known as MMR, to the autism cases. The study has been blamed for a sharp drop in the number of British children being vaccinated and for outbreaks of measles.

2004-03-05.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-05.abstract: Name listed of American soldier newly confirmed dead in Iraq; 550 US service members have died there since start of war

2004-03-05.title: Despite New G.I. Tactics, Bombs Are Still Biggest Peril
2004-03-05.abstract: US forces have used array of new technology and tactics to become more adept at identifying and disarming roadside bombs in Iraq, but devices remain biggest killer of US military personnel; at least 104 of 379 troops killed by hostile fire were killed by improvised bombs, including ten killed in February alone; Gen John P Abizaid explains efforts to prevent deaths, testimony to House Armed Services Committee; Pentagon is sending 150 robotic vehicles to defuse bombs and special armored cars and is working on using airborne sensors to detect ambushes; photos; chart (M)

2004-03-05.title: Blair Argues for Tough New Legal Standard to Fight Terror
2004-03-05.abstract: The possibility that Islamic militants will collaborate with states that possess unconventional weapons to carry out acts of terror justifies the new standard, he said.

2004-03-05.title: Rowan Atkinson : Correction
2004-03-05.abstract: An item on Jan. 2 referred to a report on an English Web site that the comedian Rowan Atkinson was being treated for depression in Arizona and was upset at the critical failure of his last film, "Johnny English." We fully accept assurances by his solicitors that Atkinson has never suffered from depression and has not been treated for depression in an Arizona clinic. We are happy to put the record straight and express regret for any upset caused to Atkinson.

2004-03-05.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Search Of Rail Network Finds No Bombs
2004-03-05.abstract: SNCF, state-run French railway, finishes search of entire 20,000 miles of track without discovering bombs threatened to be placed along vast system; unknown criminal had threatened to explode multiple bombs along rails unless more than $5 million was paid (S)

2004-03-06.title: The Collections / Paris : Chanel sends out the boys with the girls
2004-03-06.abstract: PARIS—  Karl Lagerfeld has traveled a long 21-year road with Chanel and his show on Friday took him both back to Coco's start — and forward to the future.

2004-03-06.title: Drugstore's return gives lift to Parisian shopping circuit
2004-03-06.abstract: PARIS—  Parisians are spoiled for choice this season with the opening of a wide variety of stores. But perhaps the most dramatic retail event in town is not a new shop but the reopening of a classic: the Drugstore Publicis just a short walk from the Arc de Triomphe.

2004-03-06.title: Bush Is Considering Waiver For Mexicans Entering U.S.
2004-03-06.abstract: Pres Bush welcomes Mexican Pres Vicente Fox to his ranch in Crawford, Tex, for talks on variety of issues; White House says it is considering plan that would exempt many Mexicans from strict new requirements that all visitors to US be fingerprinted and photographed (M)

2004-03-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: The Hague: Move Against Milosevic Charges
2004-03-06.abstract: Defense team, appointed by United Nations to ensure that former Yugoslav President Slobodan Milosevic, who is representing himself, receives fair trial, files motion to have genocide charges dropped for lack of evidence (S)

2004-03-06.title: Libya Discloses Production Of 23 Tons of Mustard Gas
2004-03-06.abstract: Libya, in formal declaration to Organization for Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, discloses that it had produced and stored some 23 tons of deadly mustard gas over decade ago; State Dept-chartered ship loaded with over 500 metric tons of equipment from Libya's nuclear and other weapons programs prepares to leave Libya (M)

2004-03-07.title: Raid in Gaza Fails, Killing Palestinians, Not Israelis
2004-03-07.abstract: Hamas, Islamic Jihad and Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades fail in attack on Israeli soldiers at Erez crossing point from Gaza Strip; Palestinians use three jeeps painted to resemble Israeli military vehicles, but first and third jeeps explode prematurely, and soldiers kill driver and passenger in second; casualties are all Palestinian, mostly policemen who tried to stop jeeps; thousands of Palestinians cross every day through Erez to jobs in Israel or work in industrial zone at crossing (M)

2004-03-07.title: Russian Team Of Scientists Is Rescued From Ice Floe
2004-03-07.abstract: Team of a dozen Russian scientists on ice floe 400 miles from North Pole is rescued by helicopters flown from Norwegian island of Spitsbergen; scientists were dropped at base, North Pole 32, in April 2003 and drifted in 1,700-mile loop until ice broke up their camp this week; Russia is only country to make practice of year-round ice observatories atop Arctic Ocean, but this was Russia's first such mission since collapse of Soviet Union (S)

2004-03-07.title: 9 Militants Killed In an Afghan Clash
2004-03-07.abstract: American Special Operations soldiers and Afghan National Army troops killed nine armed men suspected of being Islamic militants near the border with Pakistan on Friday, American military officials said Saturday.     The firefight occurred when roughly 40 armed men tried to flank an American sniper position east of an American base in Orgun-e, in Paktika Province, the officials said.

2004-03-07.title: At Least 6 Killed in Gunfire After March in Haiti
2004-03-07.abstract: Five Haitians and a foreign news photographer were confirmed dead when gunfire broke out after a march involving thousands.

2004-03-07.title: Native Canadians Seek Ways of Healing
2004-03-07.abstract: Native Canadians use mix of Western psychology and traditional religious ceremonies to heal traumas that many people link to attendance at government-funded residential schools; Native Canadians make up 3 percent of population but 25 percent of convicted sex offenders; government has set up Aboriginal Healing Foundation with $250 million to study legacy of schools and disburse grants for healing projects, but seeks to share burden of responsibility with churches that ran many of these schools; photos, map (M)

2004-03-08.title: Cartoon heroines get a fresh scent of power
2004-03-08.abstract: LONDON—  Move over, Stella McCartney. A trio of plucky, opinionated girls with even more attitude than the feisty Ms. McCartney are the new kids on the fashion industry block.

2004-03-08.title: Chinese SARS Hero Urges Party To Admit Error for '89 Massacre
2004-03-08.abstract: Dr Jiang Yangong, retired military physician who became folk hero when he helped expose China's initial cover-up of SARS outbreak last year, is now urging Communist Party to admit it was gravely wrong in violently cracking down on Tiananmen Square pro-democracy protests in 1989; holds most Chinese feel use of military force was wrong, but are afraid to speak out; he confirms writing letter to party, phone interview; letter calls students attacked patriots protesting official corru

2004-03-08.title: Greek Conservatives' Victory Ends Long Rule by Socialists
2004-03-08.abstract: Conservatives sweep to power in parliamentary elections in Greece, ending over decade of Socialist rule; George Papandreou concedes defeat for his party after exit polls and early results give solid lead to center-right New Democracy; New Democracy leader Costas Caramanlis vows to deliver 'immediately' on his campaign pledges for social reforms; appeals for Papandreou's support; both leaders pledge support for Athens Olympics, where preparations lag (M)

2004-03-08.title: 14 Palestinians Killed in Battle As Israelis Raid Camps in Gaza
2004-03-08.abstract: Israeli armored forces raid outskirts of two neighboring Gaza Strip refugee camps, igniting battle that lasts for hours and leaves 14 Palestinians dead and 83 hurt; at least 10 of dead are gunmen, but Palestinians say 3 are unarmed youths; no Israelis are hurt; mosque loudspeakers call gunmen to jihad; hundreds of children and teenagers, some of them flinging stones from improvised slings, swarm uncertain edges of congested battlefield; Israeli forces say they seek arms and for militants responsible for mortar and missile attack on nearby Jewish settlements; eventually withdraw under what they call extraordinarily heavy Palestinian fire, without making any arrests or seizing arms; map; photos (M)

2004-03-08.title: U.N. Official Expects Return of Millions of African Refugees
2004-03-08.abstract: The United Nations said today that the end of wars in a number of African nations should lead to a mass migration home of people who have long been languishing in refugee and displacement camps across the continent.

2004-03-09.title: A Chirac (She, Not He) Is Campaigning for Re-election
2004-03-09.abstract: Bernadette Chirac, wife of French Pres Jacques Chirac, who has been local official in Correze for 25 years, opens re-election drive; has made her mark on area in concrete but small ways; does not seem to need or want her husband to campaign with her; map; photos (M)

2004-03-09.title: Reigate Journal; Parrot May Have Been Churchill's, but She's Not Saying
2004-03-09.abstract: Charlie the parrot, who now lives at Reigate, England, garden center, declines comment on press report that she was once owned by Winston Churchill and curses with Churchillian inflection; Peter Oram, bird's owner, says he and his late father-in-law, pet shop owner who sold bird to Churchill, were asked to collect Charlie and some other birds after Churchill's death in 1965; photos (M)

2004-03-09.title: Zimbabwe Reports Seizing Plane With 64 Suspected Mercenaries
2004-03-09.abstract: Zimbabwe reports impounding United States-registered cargo plane refueling at Harare airport after its owners lied about existence of 64 suspected mercenaries and military equipment on board; US denies plane is linked to US government; passengers and crew members are taken to military base (M)

2004-03-09.title: Beijing's Criticism of Democracy Advocates Divides Hong Kong
2004-03-09.abstract: Beijing's heavy criticism of Hong Kong democracy advocates in past two weeks is increasingly dividing Hong Kong public; many worry democratic movement has gone too far, while others want to press on; division is visible when unruly throng of supporters and detractors, in unusual confrontation, greet Democratic Party founder Martin Lee at airport on his return from Washington to testify on democratic reforms before Senate panel; senior Communist officials seem to have put movement on defensive by repeatedly questioning patriotism of democracy advocates; effort has begun upsetting social stability Chinese leaders also prize (M)

2004-03-09.title: The Collections / PARIS : Taking a Silk Road, Ford's fine farewell
2004-03-09.abstract: PARIS—  With the high polish and vivid hues of Chinese lacquer, Tom Ford's valedictory collection for Yves Saint Laurent was a smash. He showed not just a new finesse that suggested how he might have taken the brand forward, had he stayed as designer. By taking the Silk Road, Ford also showed his acute instinct for the moment. China and its future role in global fashion is a current hot topic.

2004-03-10.title: Color It Green, and See How It Fills Politicians' Pockets
2004-03-10.abstract: Mexican political reforms enacted in 1996 to increase number of parties have had perverse effect of spreading corruption on scale that even jaded Mexicans find breathtaking; tapes showing leading politicians, such as Jorge Emilio Gonzalez of Green Party, involved in egregious acts of veniality described; photos; National Socialist Party, which failed to win even two percent of vote in midterm elections, nevertheless enriched founder Gustavo Riojas, now in hiding; Green Party is dominated not by environmentalists but by associates of founder Jorge Gonzalez Torres, whose son was caught on tape negotiating $2-million bribe from resort developer (M)

2004-03-10.title: 'McFashion' puts squeeze on middle market
2004-03-10.abstract: LONDON—  Fashion used to be simple for Britain's middle classes: there wasn't much of it around. So for most middle-class shopper-mothers, this meant Marks & Spencer, Britain's biggest clothing retailer, which has a store on every decent high street in Britain, selling M&S label merchandise from suits to swimwear.

2004-03-10.title: Philippine city makes its mark in global furniture design
2004-03-10.abstract: HONG KONG—  It is easy to be underwhelmed by Cebu, the Philippines' second-largest city. The tourism brochures boast sun-drenched beaches lined with coconut trees, but those who venture outside the resorts find a different place. The sun, sand and tropical fruit are certainly part of what is Cebu, but the general reality is that it is an industrial town. Cebu lives off the trade that goes on inside the squat, uninspired and often unattractive buildings of the downtown business district, and off the cargo hauled on the backs of the large trucks that rumble through its main streets.

2004-03-10.title: Shiites May Demand Lifting Of Limits on Their Power
2004-03-10.abstract: Iraq's most powerful Shiite leaders keep up pressure for changes in interim constitution, hinting that they may hinder US timetable by pushing for fewer constraints on Shiite majority; Abdel Aziz al-Hakim says new charter must remove impediments to Shiite power, and Grand Ayatollah Muhammad Taqi al-Modaresi is even more blunt, suggesting that interim constitution may be half-step at best (M)

2004-03-10.title: World Briefing: Americas
2004-03-10.abstract: MEXICO: Prosecutor In Crime-Torn State Quits; GUATEMALA: House Arrest For Ex-Dictator;.

2004-03-11.title: Pentagon Pays Iraq Group, Supplier of Incorrect Spy Data
2004-03-11.abstract: Pentagon is paying $340,000 a month to Iraqi political organization led by Ahmad Chalabi for 'intelligence collection,' even though much of its information before US invasion was useless, misleading or fabricated; CIA reportedly must get Pentagon permission before interviewing informants from Iraqi National Congress, which has close ties to Bush administration (M)

2004-03-11.title: Alarm Raised Over Quality Of Uranium Found in Iran
2004-03-11.abstract: UN nuclear inspectors have found traces of extremely highly enriched uranium in Iran, of purity reserved for use in nuclear bomb; finding increases urgency of determining uranium's source (S)

2004-03-11.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-03-11.abstract: EASIER TO BE EUROPEAN; FRANCE: Railway Bomb Scare; GERMANY: Corpse Exhibitor Cleared;.

2004-03-11.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Rabbis Strike
2004-03-11.abstract: 3,000 rabbis across Israel go on strike to protest government's failure to pay salaries over past few months and in some cases over past year; photo (S)

2004-03-11.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Syria: Retort To U.S.
2004-03-11.abstract: Syria responds to US complaint about detainment of junior American diplomat who was at scene of human rights demonstration in Damascus (S)

2004-03-12.title: New Security Worries for Europes Sprawling Rail System
2004-03-12.abstract: European officials have expressed increasing concern in recent months that rail networks would be targeted by terrorists.

2004-03-12.title: World Briefing | Americas: Chile: Divorce Legalized
2004-03-12.abstract: Chilean Congress passes bill legalizing divorce after one year separation (S)

2004-03-12.title: Spain Struggles to Cope As the Equation of Terror Changes in an Instant
2004-03-12.abstract: Spaniards struggle to cope with scale of death and devastation from bombings on four commuter trains in Madrid; photos; country used to Basque terrorism is shocked by indiscriminate nature of attack on ordinary people going to work or school; at least 192 people died; Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar calls for mass rallies, but many Spaniards who opposed his support for Pres Bush's war in Iraq say that if attack came from Al Qaeda protests could shift from anti-ETA to anti-government (M)

2004-03-12.title: 10 Bombs Shatter Trains in Madrid, Killing 192
2004-03-12.abstract: Ten bombs rip through four commuter trains in Madrid during morning rush hour, killing at least 192 people and wounding more than 1,400 in deadliest terrorist attack on European target since World War II; Spanish authorities initially blame Basque separatist group ETA but later add possibility of Islamic terrorism after finding stolen van with detonators and Koran tape; US intelligence is skeptical about Al Qaeda link claimed in letter to Arab paper in London; Spain has 1,300 troops in Iraq and was explicitly threatened in Osama bin Laden audiotape; attack, three days before election, cuts train in two at main Atocha station in heart of city; bloodied victims crawl through wreckage and bodies are found burned in seats; rescue workers there and at two nearby stations struggle to separate wounded from dead; Spanish official says bomb was titadine, compressed dynamite found in van headed for Madrid last month; two ETA suspects were arrested; police in December also foiled plot to bomb train in Madrid (M)

2004-03-12.title: 2 Iraqi Women Working for U.S. Are Shot to Death in Taxi
2004-03-12.abstract: Two Iraqi washerwomen working for US forces in Basra are shot to death by masked gunmen who attack taxi; driver is spared; photo; murder renews fears that support staff, especially women, working for Americans are easy targets; murders of translator and his relatives and three other washerwomen cited (M)

2004-03-13.title: Mexico Is Warned of Risk From Altered Corn
2004-03-13.abstract: Commission for Environmental Cooperation says genetically engineered corn has made its way into Mexican fields from modified American seeds and could ultimately displace native corn varieties unless government moves to protect them (M)

2004-03-13.title: Attacks will sway vote, but how?
2004-03-13.abstract: PARIS:—  The question of whether Al Qaeda played a role in the Madrid blood bath that Spain says is almost certainly the work of the Basque group ETA is a vast and awkward issue that could have considerable impact on Sunday's national election.

2004-03-13.title: Iraqi Policemen Tied to Killing Of 2 Americans
2004-03-13.abstract: US officials say that four men arrested in connection with killing of Fern L Holland and Robert J Zangis, American civilians working for US occupation authority, were apparently members of new 70,000-member US-trained Iraqi police force; fifth man seized in killings was former member of police force under Saddam Hussein, while sixth man was civilian; killings occurred on road near Shiite holy city of Karbala; slayings have shocked Americans, who face possibility that men to whom they turn f or cooperation in fighting insurgency may include insurgent infiltrators or those paid to do their work (M)

2004-03-13.title: Madrid Convention Center Becomes a City of the Fallen
2004-03-13.abstract: Ifema convention center has been transformed into giant temporary morgue in aftermath of terrorists train bombings in Madrid; photo (M)

2004-03-13.title: Canada's Liberals May Be Learning How the West Is Lost
2004-03-13.abstract: Canada's Liberal Party allegedly handed out $75 million in public money to advertising firms in Quebec Province that were big contributors to party; kickback scandal almost certainly buried any hope that Prime Min Paul Martin had of making inroads in elections expected in May in four western provinces; opinion polls show that Martin is having hard time convincing Canadians, particularly westerners, that he did not know about payouts when he was finance minister in government of his predecessor Jean Chretien; photo (M)

2004-03-14.title: U.S. Announces New Offensive Against Taliban and Al Qaeda
2004-03-14.abstract: American military officials announce new operation against Taliban and Al Qaeda in eastern and southern Afghanistan; will forgo major spring offensive and adopt new tactics that place American troops in remote mountain areas and greater contact with Afghans in effort to generate better information; elements of Task Force 121, team of CIA officials and Special Operations soldiers involved in capture of Saddam Hussein, have been transferred from Iraq to Afghanistan (M)

2004-03-14.title: Bold Thai Leader Faces Growing Criticism
2004-03-14.abstract: Thai Prime Min Thaksin Shinawatra is energetic but authoritarian leader and is suspected of conflicts of interest; Thailand's economy grew by 6 percent in 2003, but companies in which Thaks1n family's Shin Corp held interest fared better than other companies; government has effectively barred liberalization of telecommunications industry, which is dominated by Shin Corp; newspapers critical of government have come under intense pressure; United States' annual human rights report criticizes Thaksin government's heavy-handed crackdown on drug-trafficking in 2003; photo (M)

2004-03-14.title: U.S. Tightens Security Measures at Iraq's Borders
2004-03-14.abstract: American civil administrator in Iraq, L Paul Bremer III, announces new security measures at border crossings to cut down on terrorist infiltration and smuggling; crossings between Iran and Iraq are cut from 19 to three; restrictions will be placed on border crossings with Syria in next phase of program; U-2 spy planes and E-8C surveillance aircraft will increase patrols; $300 million program will double Iraqi border security forces, increase training and buy equipment (M)

2004-03-14.title: A Steady Hand Promises Calm Amid the Furor In South Korea
2004-03-14.abstract: South Korean Prime Min Goh Kun pledges continuity in national policies after National Assembly impeaches Pres Roh Moo Hyun; nation remains calm, though 50,000 people stage protest in Seoul, and impeachment by opposition parties may rally supporters of Roh's Uri Party in April 15 elections; about 75 percent of respondents to three polls oppose impeachment (M)

2004-03-14.title: Following Attacks, Spains Governing Party Is Beaten
2004-03-14.abstract: Spains opposition Socialists ousted the center-right party of Prime Minister Jose Maria Aznar in a groundswell of voter anger and grief.

2004-03-15.title: Deep Unease Over the Future Gnaws at Moroccans in Spain
2004-03-15.abstract: Arrest of three Moroccans and increasing evidence of Al Qaeda involvement in Madrid terrorist attack puts Spain's Muslim population in state of profound unease; perhaps hardest hit are hundreds of thousands of Moroccans, who walk fine line between acceptance and rejection in Spain and say they are bracing for backlash; photo (M)

2004-03-15.title: U.S. Soldier Is Shot During Patrol in Haiti
2004-03-15.abstract: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti, March 15 - A United States marine was shot and wounded during a night-time patrol here, the military said today, becoming the first American casualty by hostile fire since the exiled president of the Caribbean country was ousted last month.

2004-03-15.title: Blix Says White House Had Set Mind on Iraqi Weapons
2004-03-15.abstract: The former U.N. inspector said the Bush Administration convinced itself of the existence of banned weapons before invading Iraq.

2004-03-15.title: G.I. Toll Is Rising as Insurgents Try Wilier Bombs and Tactics
2004-03-15.abstract: Six more American soldiers in Iraq are killed by homemade bombs that military officers say are becoming increasingly sophisticated and effective; one soldier is killed by roadside bomb west of Baghdad, and three more while on patrol in southeast Baghdad; attack in Tikrit day before kills two soldiers; officers say new explosives are smaller, can be planted more quickly and are being detonated from greater distances; bombs remain leading cause of American casualties since end of major combat operations on May 1; countermeasures range from surveillance planes to ground patrols who fire at suspected explosives to detonate them; photo (M)

2004-03-15.title: New Prime Minister Signals Spain Will Withdraw Troops From Iraq
2004-03-15.abstract: Spains newly elected Socialist prime minister signaled that his country would no longer march in lockstep with Washington.

2004-03-16.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: Sumatran Tiger Reportedly Near Extinction
2004-03-16.abstract: Sue Lieberman, World Wildlife Fund and Traffic director, reports that Sumatran tiger is near extinction with only 500 to 600 living in wild; stresses that Indonesia must enforce laws against poaching and endangered animal trading if animal is to survive; photo (S)

2004-03-16.title: Officials Tending to Blame Qaeda for Madrid Attack
2004-03-16.abstract: Spanish government all but drops claims that Madrid train bombings were probably work of Basque separatists; senior US intelligence officials are increasingly convinced of Islamic terrorist tie; see possible new model in which local extremists, perhaps with loose links to Al Qaeda, carry out attacks without direct Qaeda coordination; investigation and possible tie to Casablanca bombing last year discussed (M)

2004-03-16.title: Observer Team in Russia Lists Election Flaws
2004-03-16.abstract: European election observers criticize Russia's presidential election for falling short of basic democratic standards; Pres Vladimir Putin accepts congratulations from Pres Bush, Prime Min Tony Blair and other major world leaders after sweeping to entirely expected victory with 72.1 percent of vote (S)

2004-03-16.title: Nations With Troops in Iraq Make No Move to Join Spain
2004-03-16.abstract: Prime Min-elect Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero's announcement that he will withdraw Spanish troops from Iraq by midyear draws statements by other countries that they will not follow suit; Poland sees no change in its commitment, Prime Mil Silvio Berlusconi says Italy will not 'surrender,' and British Sec Jack Straw rejects notion that disengaging would give Spain immunity from future attack; list of countries and troops they have in Iraq; photos of Europeans in several cities observing three-minute silence to remember victims of Madrid train bombings (M)

2004-03-16.title: Gunmen Kill 3 U.S. Civilians In Car in Mosul
2004-03-16.abstract: Three American civilians working for private aid group are killed and two others wounded when their vehicle is raked by gunfire in Iraqi city of Mosul; two Americans killed earlier near Karbala are identified as Fern L Holland, lawyer working on women's rights, and Robert J Zangas, former Marine Corps pilot working with Iraqi news media; four suspects reportedly are members of new Iraqi police force (S)

2004-03-17.title: The Ripples From Spain Could Rock Berlusconi
2004-03-17.abstract: Italian political analysts say Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi may find himself challenged as never before as result of terrorist attacks in Madrid, election upset in Spain and proposed withdrawal of Spanish troops from Iraq; say his opponents are once again seeking to work on Italians' fears that Italy could pay for closeness of its alliance with US in Iraq (M)

2004-03-17.title: 8 Are Killed as Ethnic Fighting Flares in Kosovo
2004-03-17.abstract: United Nations officials described the clashes as the worst violence in Kosovo since the world body took over its administration almost five years ago.

2004-03-17.title: For Iraqis in Harm's Way, $5,000 and 'I'm Sorry'
2004-03-17.abstract: American military commanders in Iraq are just now reckoning with volume of civilian casualies streaming in for assistance; do not have precise figures or even estimates of number of noncombatant Iraqis killed or wounded by American-led forces, but nonprofit groups' estimates of Iraqi civilians killed range from 3,200 to more than 5,000, and that number grows as civilians continue to be cut down by American forces in checkpoint shootings and crossfires, accidents and mishaps; groups fault military for its continued use of cluster bombs; masses of grieving, injured and disfigured Iraqis line up twice a week at Baghdad Convention Center seeking treatment and compensation; Capt Jonathan Tracy forces $5,000 on man who watched his wife burn to death and his three children die after American missile hit his house a year ago, acknowledging that it cannot bring any of them back but saying that this is all US can do for now; photos (M)

2004-03-17.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-17.abstract: Defense Department releases names of three more American service members who died in Iraq

2004-03-17.title: Iraqi Leader Reaffirms U.N. Role In Transition
2004-03-17.abstract: Lakhdar Brahimi, United Nations special envoy in Iraq, says Grand Ayatollah Ali al-Sistani has dissociated himself from reports that some Shiites do not want UN to play role in Iraq's political transition (M)

2004-03-18.title: World Briefing | Africa: South Africa: Religion Honor
2004-03-18.abstract: Dr George F R Ellis, University of Cape Town (South Africa) theoretical cosmologist wins Templeton Prize honoring contributions to religion; announces that half of $1.4 million in prize money will go to help social causes in South Africa (S)

2004-03-18.title: 67 Won't Face Coup Charges In Africa Plot, Zimbabwe Says
2004-03-18.abstract: Lawyer for 67 men being held in Zimbabwe on charge of attempting coup in Equatorial Guinea says men have been charged with conspiring to murder a foreign leader; but Zimbabwean prosecutors deny statement, saying that their county's laws do not cover such an offense; confusion over charges is latest twist in bizarre case that began when Boeing 727, its cabin lights darkened, landed at Harare's international airport; officials from Zimbabwe and Equatorial Guinea have accused men aboard, mostly from southern Africa, of plotting to overthrow president of Equatorial Guinea in exhange for oil rights and $1.8 million (S)

2004-03-18.title: U.S. Official Says Spanish Government 'Mishandled' Reports on Bombing
2004-03-18.abstract: Bush administration says for first time that Spanish government mishandled early information about Madrid bombings when it played down evidence that Islamic extremists were behind plot and continued to cast suspicion on Basque separatists; Richard L Armitage, deputy secretary of state, believes Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar's government was turned out by voters in protest against its handling of terrorist event (M)

2004-03-18.title: Georgia's President Risks Showing Warlord Who's Boss
2004-03-18.abstract: Georgia Pres Mikhail Saakashvili's government is in standoff with provincial warlord Aslan Abashidze over who runs renegard province of Adzharia; Saakashvili tries to confront problem directly by trying to cross provincial border; is stopped by armed men at border; has raised possibility of armed conflict, putting his young govenment to harsh test; photo; map (M)

2004-03-18.title: World Briefing: Africa
2004-03-18.abstract: LIBERIA: Ex-President Sues Tribunal; SOUTH AFRICA: Religion Honor;.

2004-03-19.title: President Bushs Remarks
2004-03-19.abstract: The following is the text of President Bush's speech marking the anniversary the start of military action in Iraq, as transcribed by FDCH e-Media, Inc.

2004-03-19.title: A Year Later, an Iraqi Family Is Free but Wary
2004-03-19.abstract: Imaris of Baghdad, middle-class Shiite family, feel more relaxed in Iraq one year after American invasion but are wary about future; fled city during American bombing and returned to it after war only to face city plagued by looters; are free to practice their religion now that Saddam Hussein has been toppled, but are divided on just how religious they should be; photos (L)

2004-03-19.title: PAKISTANI TROOPS MAY BE CLOSING ON QAEDA'S NO. 2
2004-03-19.abstract: Pakistani military and intelligence officials believe they have surrounded Dr Ayman al-Zawahiri, Al Qaeda's No. 2 leader, in small cluster of villages near border with Afghanistan; Zawahiri is Egyptian-born surgeon who is believed to have planned some major Qaeda attacks, including those in US on Sept 11, 2001; Pakistanis' belief that they have cornered him is based on fierceness of resistance their forces are encountering and reports circulating for months that he has been in area; 7,000 Pakistani troops are in area, backed by jets, helicopters and artillery; American officials say there is behind-the-scenes support from US, including remotely piloted surveillance aircraft; photos; map (M)

2004-03-19.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Governor Injured In Shooting
2004-03-19.abstract: Jose Murat, governor of Oxaca (Mexico), survives ambush of his motorcade in which shower of gunfire wounds politician and two bodyguards (S)

2004-03-19.title: World Briefing: Europe
2004-03-19.abstract: BRITAIN: Hawking Questioned Over Injuries; BRITAIN: Terror Suspect To Leave Prison; CAVIAR PRODUCERS WARNED; RUSSIA: Apartment Toll At 58;.

2004-03-20.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-20.abstract: Defense Department confirms deaths of three service members in Iraq

2004-03-20.title: Hussein's Fall Leads Syrians To Test Government Limits
2004-03-20.abstract: Fall of Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein has spawned sort of 'what if' process in Syria in which some people are openly questioning government doctrine and challenging state oppression; it would have been inconceivable year ago for citizen of Syria, run by Baath Party of Pres Bashar al-Assad, to make documentary film with working title Fifteen Reasons Why I Hate the Baath; Omar Amiralay says watching overthrow of Hussein across border in Iraq prompted him to do just that; Syrian Baath Party remains firmly in control, ruling through emergency laws that basically suspend all civil rights, but subtle changes have begun; Syrians do not know what locked doors they can push open, but they are trying to find out; photo (M)

2004-03-20.title: NATO to Increase Peacekeepers in Troubled Kosovo
2004-03-20.abstract: NATO allies seek to shore up struggling peacekeeping operation in Kosovo as United Nations officials assess damage left by three days of ethnic violence; officials say unrest was ignited by drowning of three ethnic Albanian boys by local Serbs; France and Germany will send 400 to 600 more troops to join contingents already based in UN-governed province (M)

2004-03-20.title: World Briefing: Americas
2004-03-20.abstract: CANADA: Quebec Court Upholds Gay Marriage; CUBA: Wives Demand Dissidents Release;.

2004-03-20.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Missile Test
2004-03-20.abstract: Indian Defense Ministry announces test launch of nuclear-capable Prithvi missile improved to carry one-ton warhead 94 miles in 300 seconds (S)

2004-03-21.title: On Anniversary of a Divisive War, Italians Cry to Withdraw Troops
2004-03-21.abstract: Protesters throughout Europe staged demonstrations to mark the first anniversary of the American-led invasion of Iraq.

2004-03-21.title: Jogger Slain in Palestinian 'Mistake' Was a Student Who Straddled the Mideast Divide
2004-03-21.abstract: Israeli Arab George Khouri is shot and killed while jogging in East Jerusalem by Palestinians who mistook him for Jew; Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades offers apology to family for 'mistake'; Khouri was student of international relations at Hebrew University and planned to become lawyer like his father, who often argues on behalf of Arabs and Palestinians in land disputes with Israelis; Khouri's grandfather was killed in Palestinian bombing aimed at Israeli Jews in 1975; photos (M)

2004-03-21.title: 6 G.I.'s in Iraq Are Charged With Abuse Of Prisoners
2004-03-21.abstract: United States military charges six member of military police battalion with assault, cruelty, indecent acts and maltreatment of prisoners at Abu Ghraib prison in Iraq; 11 other soldiers have been suspended from duty while their cases are investigated; possibly far-reaching administrative review of commanders' policies toward detainees and internal procedures in Iraq's prisons is also under way; coalition forces are holding about 9,500 suspected insurgents and criminals, including just under 1,500 at Abu Ghraib (M)

2004-03-21.title: Malaysias Islamic Party Loses Ground in Elections
2004-03-21.abstract: The major Islamic party in Malaysia lost significant ground in parliamentary and state elections today as the governing coalition of Prime Minister Abdullah Badawi coasted to victory.

2004-03-21.title: Pakistan Battle Pierces Solitude Of Tribal Area
2004-03-21.abstract: More than 7,000 Pakistani troops battle 400 to 500 Islamic militants in tribal area outside Wana near border with Afghanistan, but military officials are backing away from earlier conclusion that fierceness of fighting must mean that Al Qaeda's No. 2 leader, Ayman al-Zawahiri, is in area; sheer number of militants involved in fighting would suggest that they are able to live there because they have significant, if passive, local support; government's efforts to win over tribal peoples are hindered by their deep suspicion of any kind of outsiders and gulf between tribal areas and rest of Pakistan; photo; maps (M)

2004-03-22.title: Thousands in Gaza Mourn Slain Hamas Leader and Vow Revenge
2004-03-22.abstract: Palestinians heralded Sheik Ahmed Yassin at a funeral that drew the largest crowds in Gaza in a decade.

2004-03-22.title: Delivery Delays Hurt U.S. Effort To Equip Iraqis
2004-03-22.abstract: Senior American commanders in Iraq publicly complain that delays in delivering radios, body armor and other equipment have hobbled their ability to build effective Iraqi security force that can ultimately replace United States troops there; say lag in supplying equipment, caused by contract dispute, may have contributed to loss of lives among Iraqi recruits; equipment for America's combat troops and that for Iraqi security services is obtained through separate contracting and procurement processes; senior Army officials in Washington say sloppy contract language, staff turnover, incomplete paperwork and stressful combat conditions on ground led to badly flawed process; Army is rushing to seek new bids for contract, but that could take months; in meantime, officials look to see if they can use other funds and piggyback on existing contracts for weapons and other equipment; photo (M)

2004-03-22.title: Politicians In Taiwan Quarreling Over Recount
2004-03-22.abstract: Leaders of Taiwan's opposition Nationalist Party demand court-supervised recount and investigation into presidential election they lost day earlier; threaten to keep supporters in streets until their demands are met; administration of Pres Chen Shui-bian rebuffs demand and says any decision on recount must work its way through judicial system; Taiwan's High Court says decision might take months; Lien Chan, Nationalist candidate who lost election to Chen by less than quarter of percentage point of vote, says United States should be concerned about events in Taiwan because they could affect island's political stability and make it more susceptible to pressure from China; US has not yet recognized Chen as winner of election; photo of Chen and Vice Pres Annette Lu being treated at hospital after what police say was assassination attempt (M)

2004-03-22.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-22.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of American service member in Iraq, bringing total number of American service members who have died there to 576 (S)

2004-03-22.title: As Europe Hunts for Terrorists, The Hunted Press Advantages
2004-03-22.abstract: Europe has become prized target for Islamic terrorists in last 20 years; police and intelligence agents all over Europe confront growing numbers of Islamic militants who blend easily with other immigrants from North Africa, Middle East and elsewhere, as terrorists among them exploit tactical advantage; militants operate in ever-changing constellation of cells, moving freely from country to country across continent; agents tracking them are constrained by jurisdictional and bureaucratic boundaries; intelligence is far too infrequently shared, often leaving information about dangerous militants woefully incomplete; at least three of suspects in Madrid terror bombings were known to European and Arab intelligence officials for some time; one, Jamal Zougam, consorted with militant leaders from five countries, had his home searched by police, yet was never thoroughly investigated; in Madrid bombings, which killed 202 people, suicide bombers were replaced by triggering devices engineered with cheap cellphones; this suggests terrorists linked to Al Qaeda are able to modify their tactics and adopt mind-set different from one investigators thought they knew; map showing places where authorities foiled terrorist plans against European targets (L)

2004-03-23.title: Palestinians Swear Vengeance For Killing of Cleric by Israelis
2004-03-23.abstract: Sheik Ahmed Yassin, spiritual leader of militant group Hamas, is heralded as new unifying martyr by tens of thousands of Palestinians who flock to Gaza for his funeral; killing of Yassin by Israeli missile attack will almost certainly deepen divide between current Israeli and Palestinian leaderships, possibly ruling out further negotiations between them over Bush administration's sidelined peace plan; it is measure of Yassin's status as icon to Palestinians and as perceived threat to Israelis that his death seems to hold consequences for every player in conflict, from Israelis and Palestinians to Egyptians and Americans; Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel calls Yassin 'archmurderer' committed to killing Jews and destroying state of Israel, while Palestinian Prime Min Ahmed Qurei calls Yassin 'great leader'; Yasir Arafat, for whom Yassin was leading rival as national symbol, calls him 'martyr'; Israeli officials repeatedly compare Yassin to Osama bin Laden; Arab and European nations condemn killing of Yassin; Bush administration says it is 'troubled' by killing, but national security adviser Condoleezza Rice notes that Hamas is terrorist organization and Yassin is believed to have personally been involved in terrorist planning; photos (L)

2004-03-23.title: Oil for Food: Annan Seeks To Blunt Allegations
2004-03-23.abstract: United Nations Secretary Gen Kofi Annan presses reluctant members of Security Council to help investigate charges of corruption in oil-for-food program that it ran in Iraq, in effort to blunt allegations of United Nations cover-up and shore up organization's credibility; United States urged Annan to override private objections to inquiry from France and Russia, two permanent members of Security Council that figure most prominently in charges emerging from Iraq; allegations, which first arose in Iraqi newspaper in Jan, include list of companies and individuals, 46 of them Russian, as recipients of illegal allocations of oil; emerging evidence from various sources shows oil-for-food program quickly evolved into open bazaar of payoffs, favoritism and kickbacks and lax, if any, official oversight (M)

2004-03-23.title: U.S. Team in Baghdad Fights A Persistent Enemy: Rumors
2004-03-23.abstract: Rumors, however ill founded, bizarre or malevolent, present obstacle to American project to build stable democracy in Iraq; almost no tale is too outlandish to be believed by some; to counter street chatter that is not all accurate, American-led team of Iraqis gathers rumors in order to respond to them; their daily intelligence document, called The Baghdad Mosquito, is distributed via e-mail to elite group of military officers and policy planners and is posted on military's classified Web server; Iraq, which under Saddam Hussein was closed society, is ideal hothouse for rumors and gossip; presence of occupying power only adds to Iraqis' natural suspicions; photo (M)

2004-03-23.title: Taiwanese President Agrees To Recount of Disputed Vote
2004-03-23.abstract: Pres Chen Shui-bian of Taiwan agrees to recount of his disputed victory by razor-thin margin in presidential elections Mar 20, giving in to pressure from street demonstrations and United States; government says recount could start as soon as Mar 24, but could last for days because considerable work would be involved in recounting and certifying more than 13 million ballots; Chen expresses regret that anyone thinks he might have staged shooting incident day before election in which he was slightly injured (M)

2004-03-23.title: Militants Pakistan Pursues May Be Gone
2004-03-23.abstract: No concrete evidence has emerged to confirm Pakistani officials' suggestions that Dr Ayman al-Zawahir, Al Qaeda's No 2 leader, is trapped with militants in South Waziristan, focus of current Pakistani military operation; it appears that Pakistani troops sent to fiercely independent tribal areas more than two years ago failed to find militants who might have been living on their doorstep; Afghan and some American officials contend that Pakistani forces have simply not tried to find militants, or in some cases overtly aided them; photo (M)

2004-03-24.title: U.S. Law Puts World Ports on Notice
2004-03-24.abstract: US moves to prevent seaborne terrorist attack by taking port security program to farflung foreign ports, using law of the sea enacted 16 months ago, as well as parallel global code adopted by UN International Maritime Organization; law and code set July 1 deadline for all world's ships and ports to create counterterrorism systems, including computers, communications gear, surveillance cameras and patrols; ship can be turned away from US waters if it or one of last ten ports where vessel called does not meet standards; Rear Adm Larry L Hereth notes harsh economic consequences of violations; high costs and deadline have created scramble in major ports, especially in poor ones like Puerto Cortes, Honduras; daunting task of policing seas explained; map locating seaborne terrorist plots (M)

2004-03-24.title: Violence in Kosovo
2004-03-24.abstract: Report on burning of Serbian homes in Svinjare, Kosovo, noted

2004-03-24.title: Bomb Found on Rail Line in Northeastern France
2004-03-24.abstract: It was the second explosive device found since an obscure terrorist entity began threatening in December to attack Frances railroads unless it was paid millions of dollars.

2004-03-24.title: Father of Slain Afghan Minister Demands Government Action
2004-03-24.abstract: Thousands of people swarm around hilltop shrine in Herat for burial of murdered Afghan Aviation Minister Mir Wais Sadeq as his father warlord Ismail Khan demands government find killers or countenance revenge attacks; Sadeq was gunned own by soldiers serving father's rival Zaher Naibzadah (S)

2004-03-24.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: Suspected Plotters In Prison Court
2004-03-24.abstract: Seventy men accused of plotting to overthrow Equatorial Guinea's government are arraigned in Zimbabwe prison where they have been held since they and their plane were seized at airport; many are associated with mercenary organizations (S)

2004-03-25.title: Arms-Control Group Says U.S. Inflated Libya's Nuclear Bid
2004-03-25.abstract: Institute for Science and International Security, private arms-control group, says Bush administration overstated number of devices Libya had for making uranium fuel, rekindling debate over how close Libya came to acquiring nuclear bomb; says that while Libya otained 4,000 casings for uranium centrifuges, few if any had finely tooled rotors that are machine's heart; Energy Department holds Libya had parts and raw materials for making centrifuges; institute president David Albright says Libya was several years from being able to produce enough highly enriched uranium for bomb (M)

2004-03-25.title: World Leaders Converge in Spain to Mourn Bomb Victims
2004-03-25.abstract: World leaders, including Sec of State Colin L Powell, British Prime Min Tony Blair, French Pres Jacques Chirac and German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, attend official funeral in Madrid for victims of March 11 terror attacks; use opportunity to meet with Prime Min-elect Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero; King Juan Carlos and Queen Sofia go row to row to comfort victims' families; Zapatero, in private meetings with leaders, pledges to repair ties badly damaged when Europe divided over Iraq war and fulfill campaign pledge to withdraw Spain's 1,300 troops from Iraq unless they are put under United Nations mandate by end of June; avoids harsh rhetoric; tells Powell that Spain would be willing to share more responsibility in Afghanistan, where it has small troop presence; photo (M)

2004-03-25.title: Israeli Soldiers Thwart a Boy's Suicide Bombing Attempt
2004-03-25.abstract: Israeli troops on high alert at West Bank checkpoint near Nablus after finding bomb last week carried by 11-year-old boy, thwart suicide bombing attempt by Palestinian youth Hussam Abdo, who had bomb under his sweater; much of drama is recorded by television news cameraman, a Palestinian, who was among those waiting to cross checkpoint; Israel, swift to highlight Palestian use of youths barely in their teens, immediately invites journalists to scene; map (M)

2004-03-25.title: U.N. Rights Panel Condemns Israel's Killing of Hamas Leader
2004-03-25.abstract: United Nations Human Rights Commission votes, 31-2, to condemn Israel's killing of Hamas leader, Sheik Ahmed Yassin; United States, which votes against resolution, cites failure to mention Palestinian terror attacks; blocks similar resolution in Security Council; Israeli Min Silvan Shalom meets with UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan, who condemns killing, to explain Israel's action and urge UN to push moderates in region to stop protecting extremists (M)

2004-03-25.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Measure To Keep Arms From Terrorists
2004-03-25.abstract: John D Negroponte, United States ambassador to United Nations, and Britain's Ambassador Emyr Jones Parry present resolution to Security Council that would strengthen anti-terrorism efforts against terrorists not connected with particular nation (S)

2004-03-26.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: 26 Jailed In Antigovernment Plot
2004-03-26.abstract: Egyptian court sentences 23 Egyptians and 3 Britons to prison terms ranging one to five years for trying to overthrow government; contends men are members of outlawed Islamic Liberation Party (S)

2004-03-26.title: Blair Visits Qaddafi, Ending Libya's Long Estrangement
2004-03-26.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain greets Col Muammar el-Qadddafi in tent near Tripoli, officially ending Libya's three decades of isolation; they exchange promises to fight terrorism that Qaddafi once enthusiastically supporterd; Blair says he and Pres Bush seek to reward Libya for its decision, announced Dec 19, to surrender 20-year accumulation of unconventional weapons, including extensive effort aimed at making nuclear bombs; Blair is criticized by some relatives of those who died in 1988 bombing of Pan Am jet, for which Libya has admitted responsibility; Blair seems determined to make trip an expression of good faith after months of secret talks last year that led to Dec breakthrough; White House continues to encourage Qaddafi toward disarmament, although Pres Bush is in no hurry to meet with him (M)

2004-03-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Railway Bomb Threats Suspended
2004-03-26.abstract: AZF, French group that has been threatening to bomb railroads throughout France if $5 million is not paid, sends letter to President Jacques Chirac and Interior Minister Nicolas Sarkozy that says it has suspended threats against railroads (S)

2004-03-26.title: Mexico City Journal; On Mexico's Mean Streets, The Sinners Have a Saint
2004-03-26.abstract: Hundreds of men, women and children from crime-ravaged slums pack streets of Mexico City on first day of every month to pray for miracles from Santa Muerte, or 'Saint Death,' ghoul they have named an angel; describe Santa Muerte as angel of last resort for outlaws and outcasts, people who feel abandoned by their government and disparaged by church; Catholic Church has condemned Santa Muerte services as devil worship; shrines to Santa Muerte have been erected in public spaces all around Mexico; real power behind cult comes from country's impoverished and neglected masses; photos (M)

2004-03-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Protest Against Bullfighting
2004-03-26.abstract: Hundreds of animal rights activitists protest bullfighting and present petition to Barcelona city government bearing 245,000 signatures; Deputy Mayor Jordi Portabella holds City Council will consider ban on sport (S)

2004-03-27.title: After May 1, East Europe's 'Haves' May Have More
2004-03-27.abstract: Second article in series The New Europe notes that Poland will enter European Union on May 1 as one of 10 new members; never before has union invited into its ranks kind of economic malaise to be found in Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and Baltic states; Poland is dependent on small-scale agriculture and biggest challenge among group to system of farm subsidies that underpins European agriculture; new members will expand union's population from 380 million to 455 million, but they will add only 5 percent to its economy; graph; photos (M)

2004-03-27.title: Dual career couples : The travails of a commuter marriage
2004-03-27.abstract: LONDON—  Three days after our honeymoon, I left my wife in London and flew to Milan. This wasn't some fit of pique or the siren call of some classy Italian dame. No, the reason was work, which is to say money. I had been living and working in Italy for the past six months and that wasn't about to change for a couple of gold rings. At home, Gill had a growing business and an ailing mother: it was deadlock.

2004-03-27.title: Anglicans' former leader angers Muslims with criticism of Islam
2004-03-27.abstract: MILAN:—  George Carey, the former archbishop of Canterbury, has incensed Muslims by criticizing Islam for being "associated with violence throughout the world."

2004-03-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: 3 Held In Rail Bomb Threats
2004-03-27.abstract: French antiterrorism police detain two men and one woman around Paris and Loiret region for suspicious movements thought to be related to AZF; group has been threatening to bomb French railway system if demands of $5 million are not met (S)

2004-03-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Yukos Co-Owner Faces Trial
2004-03-27.abstract: Russian businessman Platon Lebedev, who was arrested last July on suspicion of fraud and embezzlement of Yukos Oil assets, will stand trial; Izvestia, newspaper, holds trial could be precursor for fate of nation's richest man Mikhail B Khodorkovsky, who is also being held and faces same charges, but is widely thought to be victim of political action; photo (S)

2004-03-28.title: Official Is Said To Recommend Sharon Charge
2004-03-28.abstract: Israeli state prosecutor Edna Arbel reportedly will recommend to Atty Gen Menachem Mazuz that Prime Min Ariel Sharon be indicted for allegedly accepting bribes from real estate developer David Appel; Appel allegedly tried to win Sharon's help in real estate deals, including one on Greek island, starting in late 1990's, when Sharon was foreign minister; allegedly paid most of $700,000 in bribes to Sharon's son, Gilad; indictment could add to Sharon's political burdens as he tries to rally support in Israel and Bush administration for his plan to unilaterally withdraw soldiers and settlers from Gaza Strip and part of West Bank; legal experts are divided about whether indictment would automatically compel him to resign; he continues to dominate Israel's politics, but his popular support has faded; photos (M)

2004-03-28.title: Mood Sours for Japan's Other Asian Students
2004-03-28.abstract: Asian students studying in Japan typically leave with bad impression of country; Japan has hosted thousands of students since 1980's, intending to teach them about Japanese culture and heal wounds of Japan's imperial past; students complain of lack of warmth and friends among Japanese; Japanese, too, are dissatisfied with program; government has tightened visa requirements; photos (M)

2004-03-28.title: Long After Guerrilla War, Survivors Demand Justice From Brazil's Government
2004-03-28.abstract: Brazilian Pres Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva's government is resisting efforts to open inquiry into Araguaia guerrilla war, fought in northeastern Brazil from 1970-1974; military reportedly massacred 60 combatants from Communist Party of Brazil and score of peasants caught in crossfire; victims' families have sought full accounting, but commission created by government is viewed as ineffective and obstructive, and da Silva's government refuses to create tribunal to judge behavior of army during military dictatorship; photos, map (M)

2004-03-28.title: North Korea Rejects U.S. Demand to Scrap Its Nuclear Programs
2004-03-28.abstract: North Korean radio explicitly rejects American demand for 'complete, verifiable, irreversible dismantling' of its nuclear programs; charges that US is secretly plotting nuclear war on North Korea; statement comes just after visit of Chinese Foreign Minister Li Zhaoxing, who said that North Korea was ready to 'push forward' with third round of talks involving US, South Korea, China, Japan and Russia; Vice Pres Cheney is due to visit region in April (M)

2004-03-28.title: A Long Fuse Links Tangier To Bombings In Madrid
2004-03-28.abstract: At least five of suspects arrested in connection with Madrid train bombings came from Tangier, and are linked to broader group of militants who returned to Morocco from Afghanistan in late 1990's; Islamic revival is strong in region, focused on Salafia Jihadia movement of Muhammad al-Fizazi, who is serving 30-year prison sentence for inciting violence in Morocco; unemployment is estimated at 30 percent among young men, and Spain is only eight miles away; many are secular when they journey to Europe in search of work, but come back radicalized; photos (M)

2004-03-29.title: G.I.'s Padlock Baghdad Paper Accused of Lies
2004-03-29.abstract: American soldiers shut down popular Baghdad newspaper and tighten chains across its doors after occupation authorities accuse it of printing lies that incite violence; thousands of outraged Iraqis protest closing of paper, radical Shiite weekly called Al Hawza; see it as act of American hypocrisy; closing of newspaper illustrates quandary Americans face in trying to strike balance between their two main goals--encouraging democracy while maintaining stability; many Iraqis say closing down newspaper will only inflame anti-occupation feelings; among Iraqi journalists, Al Hawza is known for printing wild rumors, especially anti-American ones; paper is considered mouthpiece for Moktada al-Sadr, fiery young Shiite cleric and one of most outspoken critics of Americans; letter ordering paper closed, signed by American administrator L Paul Bremer III, cites what American authorities call false reports in Al Hawza, including Feb dispatch claiming that explosion that killed 50 Iraqi police recruits was caused not by car bomb, as occupation officials say, but by American missile; photo (M)

2004-03-29.title: Sharons Son Told to Hand Over Papers in Corruption Cases
2004-03-29.abstract: Israels Supreme Court ordered Prime Minister Ariel Sharons son to hand over documents in two corruption investigations.

2004-03-29.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-29.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of service member in Iraq (S)

2004-03-29.title: Seminyak Journal; Bali's Richness Offered to the World, by Onetime Hippies
2004-03-29.abstract: Eclectic group of American, Canadian and European hippies who were drawn to Bali in 1970's by its elaborate Hindu ceremonies have stayed on, and some have become millionaire businessmen specializing in promoting Bali's culture to Westerners; most hippies-turned-businessmen live in enclaves like one in Seminyak beach village; biggest problem for these businessmen who want to fill orders is very thing that charmed them in first place: relaxed pace of life and Balinese desire to observe their religion in all its elaborate, and decorative manifestations; photo; map (M)

2004-03-29.title: A Struggle to Bring Law and Order to Haiti
2004-03-29.abstract: Members of Haiti's new interim government face challenge of resurrecting rule of law and ending official impunity; they plan to strengthen emaciated justice system and to gut and rebuild police force that is widely viewed as disorganized, demoralized, ineffectual and corrupt; interim Prime Min Gerard Latortue, who has vowed to lead nonpartisan government, will be closely watched for how he handles accused and convicted criminals among ranks of his own government's perceived allies; human rights advocates in Haiti and abroad say they will not stand by if Latortue administration abuses its own power; they are alarmed that Latortue, in visit to Gonalves, hailed as 'freedom fighters' rebels who had been jailed for killings of Aristide supporters in 1994 (M)

2004-03-30.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Fox Sends Congress Justice Reforms
2004-03-30.abstract: Mexican President Vicente Fox sends Congress package of sweeping reforms aimed at improving nation's outdated criminal justice system (S)

2004-03-30.title: 19 Killed in Uzbekistan; Terrorism Blamed
2004-03-30.abstract: Series of terrorist incidents in Tashkent and Bukhara in Uzbekistan kills 19 people and wounds at least 26 others; Uzbek prosecutor general Rashid Kadyrov says there were two bombings, two attacks on police officers and explosion that killed 10 at building where bombs were being assembled; blames Islamic militants who have been target of harsh crackdowns in Uzbekistan, where United States has important military base; Uzbekistan has carried out campaign of repression against Muslim dissidents; its human rights record has been criticized by Human Rights Watch and by International Crisis Group; map (M)

2004-03-30.title: More Deaths in Uzbekistan as Police and Militants Battle
2004-03-30.abstract: At least 20 people were reported dead today in Uzbekistan, a strategic ally of the U.S. that borders Afghanistan.

2004-03-30.title: 8 Terror Suspects Arrested in England
2004-03-30.abstract: British police confiscated about 1,000 pounds of ammonium nitrate fertilizer, the key ingredient in making explosives.

2004-03-30.title: Names of the Dead
2004-03-30.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2004-03-31.title: A New Future for Spain: Call It Social Socialism
2004-03-31.abstract: Spain's incoming Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero, who has made headlines with plan to withdraw troops from Iraq, maps social and sexual revolution at home with plans to legalize some form of gay marriage, rid public schools and medical research of Catholic dogma, create nonpartisan state television and enact laws eradicating sexism in society; photos; Spain already has legal prostitution, major pornography industry, flamboyant gay community and tolerance of private drug use, but Zapatero also wants purge of residue of conservatism that marked Jose Maria Aznar's eight-year administration (M)

2004-03-31.title: U.N. Court Orders U.S. to Review Cases of Mexicans
2004-03-31.abstract: The International Court of Justice ordered that American courts review death sentences imposed on 51 Mexicans in the U.S.

2004-03-31.title: Bush Administration Calls Attacks Despicable
2004-03-31.abstract: The White House and the State Department condemned the attacks today on Americans in Iraq, calling them despicable deeds carried out by people on the wrong side of history.

2004-03-31.title: Police in Uzbekistan Arrest Dozens, Reports Say
2004-03-31.abstract: The police in Uzbekistan made dozens of arrests today in an effort to stem a wave of violence that included reports of at least one new explosion.

2004-03-31.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Lithuanian Diplomats Expelled
2004-03-31.abstract: Russia, retaliating for Lithuania's recent expulsion of three Russian diplomats, orders three Lithuanian diplomats to leave Russia; Lithuanian officials contend that Russians were trying to obtain sensitive information about impeachment process of President Rolandas Paksas (S)
